channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Iran supreme leader warns of ‘unforgettable lessons’ if US attacks continue
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei says Trump's signature is 'worthless' and that 'bullying' is a core element of US foreign policy.

item.title: Israeli attacks on residential areas in Gaza kill eight
item.description: Attacks hit neighbourhoods in Gaza City far beyond the line Israeli forces are meant to hold under the ceasefire.

item.title: New York’s Mamdani says looking into Netanyahu arrest during city visit
item.description: Mamdani had pledged to arrest Netanyahu during campaign for mayor and says he is consulting city's legal advisers.

item.title: Damage visible at Iran bridges and water plant after US attacks
item.description: US strikes damaged bridges and a water desalination plant in Iran after a seventh straight night of attacks.

item.title: US military says two service members killed in Iranian strike in Jordan
item.description: US and Iran have stepped up attacks in the last week, prompting alarm about return to full-scale war across the region.

item.title: France vs England LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026 third-place playoff
item.description: Live coverage and text updates from our commentary stream of the Bronze Final as Mbappe's France play Kane's England.

item.title: Lebanon’s Aoun to meet Trump in Washington to discuss Israel talks
item.description: The Lebanese and US presidents are expected to discuss the ceasefire, southern Lebanon, and Israel’s withdrawal.

item.title: UK’s Josh Kerr smashes longstanding world mile record in London
item.description: Kerr ran a 3:42.66 mile at the London Diamond League, smashing Hicham El Guerrouj’s world record by 0.47 seconds.

item.title: What to expect after the US reimposes naval blockade on Iran’s ports?
item.description: Pressure is expected to build on the Iranian public, as well as global energy markets due to the US blockade.

item.title: Armed groups attack Malian military convoy in Gao region
item.description: Mali says counterattack under way as rebels claim they inflicted 'human losses' and 'material damage' on troops.

item.title: Belgium bans imports from Israeli settlements in occupied Palestine
item.description: The move comes as EU foreign ministers remain deadlocked over a bloc-wide ban on illegal settlement trade.

item.title: Venezuela quake death toll tops 5,000 as IMF releases emergency aid
item.description: IMF releases £346m in emergency funds as new details emerge about the disaster response.

item.title: Plumes of smoke rise over Kuwait after repeated Iranian attacks
item.description: Towering plumes of smoke rose over Kuwait after Iranian strikes hit multiple locations in Mangaf.

item.title: Iraq signs 48 deals with US companies during PM’s visit to Washington
item.description: The deals include rebuilding the long-defunct Iraq-Syria crude oil pipeline, which could bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: China warns UK over British Steel nationalisation, demands fair resolution
item.description: Jingye seeks compensation after UK's move to fully nationalise loss-making British Steel, citing security risks.

item.title: Top German conservative quits after surrogacy sparks backlash within party
item.description: Jens Spahn steps down after party criticism over surrogacy, highlighting tensions in Germany's conservative coalition.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Spain vs Argentina – What to know ahead of the final
item.description: Lamine Yamal and Lionel Messi meet in a clash of generations in Sunday's blockbuster Spain vs Argentina World Cup final.

item.title: Death toll from Uganda school bus crash rises to 24
item.description: Three more pupils died after Uganda’s school bus crash, raising the death toll to 23 students and one adult.

item.title: Iran suggests MoU ‘suspended’ as 50 killed in US strikes in July
item.description: Conflict has been escalating since US President Donald Trump declared 10 days ago that peace deal with Iran was over.

item.title: Ukrainian strikes hit Russian logistics center
item.description: Ukrainian strikes hit Russian logistics center

item.title: Europe should look to Africa for heatwave solutions
item.description: African communities have spent decades developing practical, tested ways to live with extreme heat.

item.title: India achieves milestone with launch of first private-sector orbital rocket
item.description: PM Narendra Modi says successful launch will 'encourage countless youngsters to dream bigger and innovate fearlessly'.

item.title: India’s salt workers: The human cost of the world’s most popular seasoning
item.description: 101 East investigates the supply chain behind India’s booming salt industry.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia kill at least 8, wound dozens
item.description: Russian officials say strikes on retailer's warehouses caused civilian casualties; Kyiv says sites used to make drones.

item.title: Indian police forcibly hospitalise activist after 20-day hunger strike
item.description: Police said protesters tried to stop them from seizing Sonam Wangchuk, causing a 'slight commotion'.
